name: Irreversible actions require human approval regardless of agent confidence
description: Actions that cannot be undone — permanent deletion, table drops, published releases, sent communications, financial transactions — require explicit human approval before execution. No level of agent confidence bypasses this gate.

policy.rationale: Reversibility is a prerequisite for autonomous action. When an action cannot be reversed, the cost of a mistake is unbounded. Human approval is the minimum acceptable gate regardless of chain-of-thought length.
